Structure: N, + who/which + do/does not + V, ...
Meaning (VI): M?nh d? quan h? không xác d?nh (có d?u ph?y)
Grammar Example: The Eiffel Tower, which was built in 1889, is iconic.

Structure: (Không dùng 'that'; thu?ng trong van vi?t)
Meaning (VI): M?nh d? quan h? không xác d?nh (có d?u ph?y)
Grammar Example: My boss, who never smiles, gave me a compliment today.

Structure: S + V + adj-er + than + N / S + V + more + adj + than + N
Meaning (VI): So sánh hon – tính t? ng?n thêm -er, dài dùng more
Grammar Example: She is taller than her brother.

Structure: S + V + not + as + adj + as + N
Meaning (VI): So sánh hon – tính t? ng?n thêm -er, dài dùng more
Grammar Example: This book is not as interesting as that one.

Structure: Is + S + adj-er + than + N?
Meaning (VI): So sánh hon – tính t? ng?n thêm -er, dài dùng more
Grammar Example: Is this phone more expensive than that one?

Structure: S + V + the + adj-est / S + V + the most + adj
Meaning (VI): So sánh nh?t – dùng the + -est ho?c the most
Grammar Example: He is the tallest student in the class.

Structure: S + V + not + the + adj-est / not + the most + adj
Meaning (VI): So sánh nh?t – dùng the + -est ho?c the most
Grammar Example: This is not the most expensive restaurant in town.

Structure: What/Who is the + adj-est / the most + adj?
Meaning (VI): So sánh nh?t – dùng the + -est ho?c the most
Grammar Example: What is the most popular sport in Vietnam?

Structure: Both A and B + V(pl) / Not only A but also B + V
Meaning (VI): Nh?n m?nh hai d?i tu?ng cùng lúc
Grammar Example: Both Tom and Mary are coming to the party.

Structure: (Dùng neither...nor d? ph? d?nh c? hai)
Meaning (VI): Nh?n m?nh hai d?i tu?ng cùng lúc
Grammar Example: Neither Tom nor Mary is coming to the party.

Structure: Do both A and B + V?
Meaning (VI): Nh?n m?nh hai d?i tu?ng cùng lúc
Grammar Example: Are both Tom and Mary coming to the party?

Structure: Either A or B + V (chia theo B)
Meaning (VI): L?a ch?n (either/or) ho?c ph? d?nh c? hai (neither/nor)
Grammar Example: Either you or he is responsible for this.

Structure: Neither A nor B + V (chia theo B)
Meaning (VI): L?a ch?n (either/or) ho?c ph? d?nh c? hai (neither/nor)
Grammar Example: Neither the manager nor the staff was informed.

Structure: Is it either A or B?
Meaning (VI): L?a ch?n (either/or) ho?c ph? d?nh c? hai (neither/nor)
Grammar Example: Is it either tea or coffee that you prefer?

Structure: Although/Even though + S + V, S + V
Meaning (VI): Di?n t? s? nhu?ng b? – m?nh d? trái nghia
Grammar Example: Although it was raining, we went for a walk.

Structure: Although + S + do/does not + V, S + V
Meaning (VI): Di?n t? s? nhu?ng b? – m?nh d? trái nghia
Grammar Example: Although she did not study much, she passed the exam.

Structure: Even though + S + V, does + S + still + V?
Meaning (VI): Di?n t? s? nhu?ng b? – m?nh d? trái nghia
Grammar Example: Even though he is rich, does he still live simply?

Structure: Despite/In spite of + N/V-ing, S + V
Meaning (VI): Nhu?ng b? – theo sau là danh t? ho?c V-ing (không có S+V)
Grammar Example: Despite the rain, we went for a walk.

Structure: Despite/In spite of + not + V-ing, S + V
Meaning (VI): Nhu?ng b? – theo sau là danh t? ho?c V-ing (không có S+V)
Grammar Example: In spite of not feeling well, she went to work.

Structure: Despite + N/V-ing, does + S + still + V?
Meaning (VI): Nhu?ng b? – theo sau là danh t? ho?c V-ing (không có S+V)
Grammar Example: Despite being tired, did she finish the project?

Structure: S + be + too + adj + to + V / S + be + adj + enough + to + V
Meaning (VI): too...to = quá...nên không; enough...to = d?...d?
Grammar Example: He is too tired to drive. / She is strong enough to lift it.

Structure: S + be + not + adj + enough + to + V
Meaning (VI): too...to = quá...nên không; enough...to = d?...d?
Grammar Example: He is not old enough to vote.

Structure: Is + S + too + adj + to + V? / Is + S + adj + enough + to + V?
Meaning (VI): too...to = quá...nên không; enough...to = d?...d?
Grammar Example: Is it too hot to go outside? / Is she tall enough to reach it?

Structure: S + V + so + adj/adv + that + S + V / S + V + such + (a/an) + adj + N + that + S + V
Meaning (VI): so...that / such...that = d?n m?c mà...
Grammar Example: It was so cold that we stayed inside.

Structure: S + V + so + adj + that + S + could not + V
Meaning (VI): so...that / such...that = d?n m?c mà...
Grammar Example: It was so dark that she could not see anything.

Structure: Was + S + so + adj + that + S + V?
Meaning (VI): so...that / such...that = d?n m?c mà...
Grammar Example: Was the film so boring that you fell asleep?

Structure: a/an + danh t? d?m du?c s? ít (l?n d?u d? c?p) / the + danh t? xác d?nh
Meaning (VI): a/an = chua xác d?nh; the = dã xác d?nh; zero = chung chung
Grammar Example: I saw a dog in the park. The dog was very friendly.

Structure: Không dùng m?o t?: danh t? không d?m du?c, s? nhi?u chung chung
Meaning (VI): a/an = chua xác d?nh; the = dã xác d?nh; zero = chung chung
Grammar Example: I don't drink milk. / Dogs are loyal animals. (zero article)

Structure: Is this a/the + N? / Do you have a/the + N?
Meaning (VI): a/an = chua xác d?nh; the = dã xác d?nh; zero = chung chung
Grammar Example: Is there a supermarket near here? / Where is the nearest hospital?

Structure: many/few + N d?m du?c / much/little + N không d?m du?c
Meaning (VI): Danh t? d?m du?c dùng many/few; không d?m du?c dùng much/little
Grammar Example: There are many students in the class. There is much water in the bottle.

Structure: not many + N d?m du?c / not much + N không d?m du?c
Meaning (VI): Danh t? d?m du?c dùng many/few; không d?m du?c dùng much/little
Grammar Example: There are not many apples left. There is not much time.

Structure: How many + N d?m du?c? / How much + N không d?m du?c?
Meaning (VI): Danh t? d?m du?c dùng many/few; không d?m du?c dùng much/little
Grammar Example: How many people came? How much money do you need?

Structure: S + do/does/did + V (nguyên th?) — nh?n m?nh
Meaning (VI): Dùng do/does/did tru?c V d? nh?n m?nh s? th?t
Grammar Example: I do love you. / She does work hard. / He did call you.

Structure: (Ph? d?nh thu?ng không dùng d?ng nh?n m?nh này)
Meaning (VI): Dùng do/does/did tru?c V d? nh?n m?nh s? th?t
Grammar Example: (Không áp d?ng tr?c ti?p cho ph? d?nh nh?n m?nh)

Structure: Dùng d? ph?n bác ho?c xác nh?n m?nh
Meaning (VI): Dùng do/does/did tru?c V d? nh?n m?nh s? th?t
Grammar Example: You said I never clean — but I do clean every day!

Structure: It is/was + N/pronoun + that/who + S + V
Meaning (VI): Câu ch? – nh?n m?nh m?t thành ph?n trong câu
Grammar Example: It was Mary who broke the window.

Structure: It is not + N + that + S + V
Meaning (VI): Câu ch? – nh?n m?nh m?t thành ph?n trong câu
Grammar Example: It is not money that makes us happy.

Structure: Is it + N + that + S + V?
Meaning (VI): Câu ch? – nh?n m?nh m?t thành ph?n trong câu
Grammar Example: Is it this book that you were looking for?

Structure: S + had + been + being + V3
Meaning (VI): B? d?ng – Quá kh? hoàn thành ti?p di?n (r?t hi?m dùng)
Grammar Example: The project had been being reviewed for months before it was cancelled.

Structure: S + had + not + been + being + V3
Meaning (VI): B? d?ng – Quá kh? hoàn thành ti?p di?n (r?t hi?m dùng)
Grammar Example: The issue had not been being discussed properly.

Structure: Had + S + been + being + V3?
Meaning (VI): B? d?ng – Quá kh? hoàn thành ti?p di?n (r?t hi?m dùng)
Grammar Example: Had the problem been being monitored before it escalated?

Structure: S + will + have + been + V3
Meaning (VI): B? d?ng – Tuong lai hoàn thành
Grammar Example: The report will have been submitted by Monday.

Structure: S + will + not + have + been + V3
Meaning (VI): B? d?ng – Tuong lai hoàn thành
Grammar Example: The documents will not have been signed by then.

Structure: Will + S + have + been + V3?
Meaning (VI): B? d?ng – Tuong lai hoàn thành
Grammar Example: Will the bridge have been completed by 2026?

Structure: S + modal + be + V3
Meaning (VI): B? d?ng v?i d?ng t? khuy?t thi?u (can/must/should/may...)
Grammar Example: This problem can be solved easily.

Structure: S + modal + not + be + V3
Meaning (VI): B? d?ng v?i d?ng t? khuy?t thi?u (can/must/should/may...)
Grammar Example: Children should not be left alone at home.

Structure: Modal + S + be + V3?
Meaning (VI): B? d?ng v?i d?ng t? khuy?t thi?u (can/must/should/may...)
Grammar Example: Can this issue be resolved before the deadline?

Structure: S + told/asked + O + to + V
Meaning (VI): Câu m?nh l?nh/yêu c?u gián ti?p
Grammar Example: She told me to close the door.

Structure: S + told/asked + O + not + to + V
Meaning (VI): Câu m?nh l?nh/yêu c?u gián ti?p
Grammar Example: He asked her not to make noise.

Structure: Did + S + tell/ask + O + to + V?
Meaning (VI): Câu m?nh l?nh/yêu c?u gián ti?p
Grammar Example: Did she tell you to wait outside?

Structure: S + have/get + O + V3 / S + make/let + O + V
Meaning (VI): C?u trúc sai khi?n – nh?/b?t/d? ai làm gì
Grammar Example: I had my car repaired. / She got her nails done.

Structure: S + did not + have/get + O + V3
Meaning (VI): C?u trúc sai khi?n – nh?/b?t/d? ai làm gì
Grammar Example: He didn't let anyone enter the room.

Structure: Did + S + have/get + O + V3?
Meaning (VI): C?u trúc sai khi?n – nh?/b?t/d? ai làm gì
Grammar Example: Did you get your laptop fixed?

Structure: It's time + to V / It's high time + S + V2
Meaning (VI): Ðã d?n lúc ph?i làm gì; nh?n m?nh s? c?n thi?t
Grammar Example: It's time to go. / It's high time you studied.

Structure: It's not time + to V
Meaning (VI): Ðã d?n lúc ph?i làm gì; nh?n m?nh s? c?n thi?t
Grammar Example: It's not time to give up yet.

Structure: Is it time + to V? / Isn't it high time + S + V2?
Meaning (VI): Ðã d?n lúc ph?i làm gì; nh?n m?nh s? c?n thi?t
Grammar Example: Isn't it high time you apologized?

Structure: S + would rather + V / S + had better + V
Meaning (VI): would rather = thích hon; had better = nên (có hàm ý c?nh báo)
Grammar Example: I would rather stay home. / You had better hurry.

Structure: S + would rather not + V / S + had better not + V
Meaning (VI): would rather = thích hon; had better = nên (có hàm ý c?nh báo)
Grammar Example: She would rather not talk about it. / You had better not be late.

Structure: Would + S + rather + V (than + V)?
Meaning (VI): would rather = thích hon; had better = nên (có hàm ý c?nh báo)
Grammar Example: Would you rather eat at home or go out?

Structure: S + prefer + V-ing/N + to + V-ing/N / S + would prefer + to V
Meaning (VI): prefer = thích hon (nói chung); would prefer = thích hon (tình hu?ng c? th?)
Grammar Example: I prefer reading to watching TV. / I would prefer to stay.

Structure: S + do not prefer + V-ing / S + would prefer + not + to V
Meaning (VI): prefer = thích hon (nói chung); would prefer = thích hon (tình hu?ng c? th?)
Grammar Example: She would prefer not to attend the meeting.

Structure: Do + S + prefer + V-ing? / Would + S + prefer + to V?
Meaning (VI): prefer = thích hon (nói chung); would prefer = thích hon (tình hu?ng c? th?)
Grammar Example: Would you prefer to take a taxi or walk?

Structure: Never/Rarely/Seldom/Only + auxiliary + S + V
Meaning (VI): Ð?o ng? d? nh?n m?nh – dùng v?i tr?ng t?/c?m ph? d?nh d?ng d?u
Grammar Example: Never have I seen such beauty. / Only then did I understand.

Structure: Not only + auxiliary + S + V, but also...
Meaning (VI): Ð?o ng? d? nh?n m?nh – dùng v?i tr?ng t?/c?m ph? d?nh d?ng d?u
Grammar Example: Not only did he lie, but he also stole.

Structure: Hardly/Scarcely + had + S + V3 + when + S + V2
Meaning (VI): Ð?o ng? d? nh?n m?nh – dùng v?i tr?ng t?/c?m ph? d?nh d?ng d?u
Grammar Example: Hardly had she arrived when the phone rang.

Structure: V-ing / V3 + ..., S + V (rút g?n m?nh d? tr?ng ng?)
Meaning (VI): M?nh d? phân t? – rút g?n m?nh d? có cùng ch? ng?
Grammar Example: Seeing the dog, she screamed. / Exhausted, he sat down.

Structure: Not + V-ing + ..., S + V
Meaning (VI): M?nh d? phân t? – rút g?n m?nh d? có cùng ch? ng?
Grammar Example: Not knowing the answer, she remained silent.

Structure: (Thu?ng không dùng tr?c ti?p trong nghi v?n)
Meaning (VI): M?nh d? phân t? – rút g?n m?nh d? có cùng ch? ng?
Grammar Example: Having finished the exam, did you feel relieved?

Structure: So + auxiliary + S (d?ng ý v?i câu kh?ng d?nh)
Meaning (VI): So = tôi cung v?y (d?ng ý kh?ng d?nh); Neither = tôi cung không (d?ng ý ph? d?nh)
Grammar Example: A: I love coffee. — B: So do I.

Structure: Neither + auxiliary + S (d?ng ý v?i câu ph? d?nh)
Meaning (VI): So = tôi cung v?y (d?ng ý kh?ng d?nh); Neither = tôi cung không (d?ng ý ph? d?nh)
Grammar Example: A: I don't like spicy food. — B: Neither do I.

Structure: (Không dùng d?ng nghi v?n tr?c ti?p)
Meaning (VI): So = tôi cung v?y (d?ng ý kh?ng d?nh); Neither = tôi cung không (d?ng ý ph? d?nh)
Grammar Example: A: She can't swim. — B: Neither can he.

Structure: S + V + as + adj/adv + as + N
Meaning (VI): So sánh b?ng – hai d?i tu?ng có m?c d? nhu nhau
Grammar Example: She is as tall as her mother.

Structure: S + V + not + as + adj + as + N
Meaning (VI): So sánh b?ng – hai d?i tu?ng có m?c d? nhu nhau
Grammar Example: This film is not as good as the first one.

Structure: Is + S + as + adj + as + N?
Meaning (VI): So sánh b?ng – hai d?i tu?ng có m?c d? nhu nhau
Grammar Example: Is this laptop as fast as the new model?

Structure: The + comparative, the + comparative
Meaning (VI): Càng...càng – hai so sánh hon liên quan d?n nhau
Grammar Example: The harder you work, the more successful you become.

Structure: The less + adj + S + V, the less + adj + S + V
Meaning (VI): Càng...càng – hai so sánh hon liên quan d?n nhau
Grammar Example: The less you worry, the happier you will be.

Structure: (Thu?ng không dùng trong nghi v?n tr?c ti?p)
Meaning (VI): Càng...càng – hai so sánh hon liên quan d?n nhau
Grammar Example: The earlier you start, the sooner you finish, right?

Structure: Because/Since/As + S + V / Because of/Due to + N
Meaning (VI): Di?n t? nguyên nhân và k?t qu?
Grammar Example: She stayed home because she was sick. / Due to the rain, the match was cancelled.

Structure: Because + S + did not + V, S + V
Meaning (VI): Di?n t? nguyên nhân và k?t qu?
Grammar Example: Because he did not study, he failed the exam.

Structure: Why + did + S + V? / What caused + N?
Meaning (VI): Di?n t? nguyên nhân và k?t qu?
Grammar Example: Why did they cancel the event? Because of the storm.

Structure: so that + S + can/could + V / in order to + V / to + V
Meaning (VI): Di?n t? m?c dích c?a hành d?ng
Grammar Example: She studied hard so that she could pass. / He left early to catch the train.

Structure: so that + S + would not + V / in order not to + V
Meaning (VI): Di?n t? m?c dích c?a hành d?ng
Grammar Example: She whispered so that she would not wake the baby.

Structure: Why + did + S + V? / What did + S + V for?
Meaning (VI): Di?n t? m?c dích c?a hành d?ng
Grammar Example: Why did you call him? — To ask for help.

Structure: Therefore / As a result / Consequently / Thus, S + V
Meaning (VI): Di?n t? k?t qu?/h?u qu? c?a hành d?ng tru?c dó
Grammar Example: He didn't study. As a result, he failed the exam.

Structure: S + V. Therefore, S + could not + V
Meaning (VI): Di?n t? k?t qu?/h?u qu? c?a hành d?ng tru?c dó
Grammar Example: She was ill. Consequently, she could not attend.

Structure: (Thu?ng dùng trong van vi?t, không dùng tr?c ti?p trong nghi v?n)
Meaning (VI): Di?n t? k?t qu?/h?u qu? c?a hành d?ng tru?c dó
Grammar Example: What was the result? — Therefore, the project was delayed.

Structure: at + gi?/di?m c? th? / on + ngày/th? / in + tháng/nam/mùa
Meaning (VI): at = th?i di?m chính xác; on = ngày/th?; in = tháng/nam/mùa/kho?ng th?i gian
Grammar Example: The meeting is at 9 AM. / She was born on Monday. / He arrived in July.

Structure: S + V + not + at/on/in + th?i gian
Meaning (VI): at = th?i di?m chính xác; on = ngày/th?; in = tháng/nam/mùa/kho?ng th?i gian
Grammar Example: The shop is not open on Sundays.

Structure: When + did + S + V? / At/On/In + what time?
Meaning (VI): at = th?i di?m chính xác; on = ngày/th?; in = tháng/nam/mùa/kho?ng th?i gian
Grammar Example: At what time does the class start? / When were you born?

Structure: at + d?a di?m c? th? / on + b? m?t / in + không gian bao quanh
Meaning (VI): at = v? trí c? th?; on = trên b? m?t; in = bên trong
Grammar Example: She is at school. / The book is on the table. / He lives in Hanoi.

Structure: S + V + not + at/on/in + noi ch?n
Meaning (VI): at = v? trí c? th?; on = trên b? m?t; in = bên trong
Grammar Example: The keys are not on the desk. / He is not at home.

Structure: Where + is/are + S? / Is + S + at/on/in + N?
Meaning (VI): at = v? trí c? th?; on = trên b? m?t; in = bên trong
Grammar Example: Where is your sister? Is she at work?

Structure: some + N (kh?ng d?nh/d? ngh?) / no + N (ph? d?nh hoàn toàn)
Meaning (VI): some = m?t s? (kh?ng d?nh, d? ngh?); any = nào cung (nghi v?n/ph? d?nh); no = không có
Grammar Example: There is some milk in the fridge. / No students came late.

Structure: S + V + not + any + N (= no + N)
Meaning (VI): some = m?t s? (kh?ng d?nh, d? ngh?); any = nào cung (nghi v?n/ph? d?nh); no = không có
Grammar Example: There isn't any sugar left. / She has no time.

Structure: Is there any + N? / Would you like some + N?
Meaning (VI): some = m?t s? (kh?ng d?nh, d? ngh?); any = nào cung (nghi v?n/ph? d?nh); no = không có
Grammar Example: Is there any water left? / Would you like some tea?
